### 20260531-134132

- current task: guard schedule day-view offline visit brief cache payloads
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/app/(dashboard)/schedules/day-view.tsx`, `src/app/(dashboard)/schedules/day-view.test.tsx`, `src/lib/stores/offline-db.ts`, `src/lib/offline/crypto.ts`, related grep results for `CachedVisitBriefCard`, `visitBriefCache`, and `JSON.parse(payload)`

- bugs found: schedule day-view decoded IndexedDB visit brief cache rows with `JSON.parse(payload) as CachedVisitBriefCard`, allowing malformed roots, invalid timestamps, unsupported priority/provider values, and non-boolean fallback flags into offline/mobile visit brief rendering
- security risks found: encrypted offline visit brief cache payloads are now parsed through a shape guard before rendering; malformed fresh rows are deleted from IndexedDB instead of being retried and displayed repeatedly
- performance issues found: no material production performance issue changed; malformed fresh cache rows are pruned during the existing read pass, reducing repeated decode/parse work on later renders

- performance issues found: pending request listing is bounded; approval applies a single stock upsert inside the same transaction as the decision/audit update
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma format --schema=prisma/schema`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma generate --schema=prisma/schema`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stock-requests/route.test.ts 'src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stock-requests/[id]/route.test.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/admin/drug-masters/drug-master-content.test.tsx'`; targeted ESLint for changed request API/UI files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma db push --schema=prisma/schema`; `git diff --check`
- validation results: Prisma format/validate/generate passed; targeted Vitest passed 3 files / 9 tests, then request API regression passed 2 files / 5 tests after a JSON null type fix; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; local `ph_os_dev` db push passed; whitespace check passed

### 20260527-215220

- current task: make adopted-drug impact review totals cover all stocked formulary items instead of only the first 500 rows
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/impact/route.ts`, `src/app/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/impact/route.test.ts`, `prisma/schema/drug.prisma`, and related drug master/formulary references

- bugs found: `/api/pharmacy-drug-stocks/impact` loaded only `take: 500` stocked formulary rows before calculating all review, reorder, safety, expiry, action-required, and recent-change totals; facilities with more than 500 adopted drugs could see undercounted dashboard totals and queues

### 20260521-165010

- current task: continue WebSocket/Yjs hardening by moving collaboration room token signing secrets from raw Lambda env values to Secrets Manager ARN references with strict runtime validation
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/config/secrets.ts`, `src/lib/config/app-env.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/room-token.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/room-token.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/authorizer/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/authorizer/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.yaml`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/README.md`, `package.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, and subagent findings from security-auditor, test-auditor, and reviewer-strict
- files changed: `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/room-token.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/room-token.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/authorizer/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.yaml`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/README.md`, `package.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: SAM authorizer still received the room-token signing secret as a raw environment value; app and Lambda secret parsing accepted JSON SecretString values without the expected `COLLABORATION_ROOM_TOKEN_SECRET` key as the signing secret; short/weak signing secrets were accepted after ARN migration; Lambda verifier did not have first-class tests for Secrets Manager success/failure/direct-env rejection; app production-like detection was initially tied to `NODE_ENV` instead of the repo's `APP_ENV` convention
- security risks found: reduced signing-key exposure by changing the SAM contract to pass only `COLLABORATION_ROOM_TOKEN_SECRET_ARN` and grant authorizer-scoped `secretsmanager:GetSecretValue`; reduced secret misconfiguration risk by requiring raw or JSON secret values to resolve to a trimmed 32+ character signing secret and rejecting the known local fallback value; Lambda validation now fails closed on Secrets Manager lookup failure, missing key, malformed JSON, short secret, and direct env values in Lambda-like runtimes; app issuer uses `APP_ENV`/`AWS_EXECUTION_ENV` production-like gating rather than `NODE_ENV`
- performance issues found: added a 5-minute in-process Secrets Manager cache for app and Lambda secret lookups, matching the room-token TTL to limit repeated AWS calls while bounding rotation drift
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false add @aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager@^3.1050.0` exited 1 after updating dependencies because existing pnpm ignored-build policy requires approval; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts`; Prettier on touched token/template/README/package files; targeted Vitest for app room-token, Lambda room-token, authorizer, and template tests; full WebSocket Lambda/template Vitest plus collaboration room-token service test; targeted ESLint for touched token/template tests and handlers; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --audit-level high`; `command -v sam && command -v cfn-lint`
- validation results: targeted room-token/template suite passed 4 files / 34 tests; WebSocket Lambda/template suite passed 8 files / 58 tests; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; diff whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed 542 files / 2691 tests with existing jsdom navigation warnings only; full lint passed; Next.js 16.2.6 production build passed with 217 app routes; dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities; `sam` and `cfn-lint` were not installed, so SAM deployability validation remains unrun
- remaining work: add an actual SAM/cfn-lint deployability gate and possibly Node 20 Lambda runtime matrix; express the Next app runtime `COLLABORATION_ROOM_TOKEN_SECRET_ARN` and `secretsmanager:GetSecretValue` contract in whichever app deployment IaC owns the Next service; decide whether to add optional CMK `kms:Decrypt`; implement client token renewal before the 5-minute expiry and broader revocation/version checks
- next action: continue the broader bug ledger with deployability/CI gates or client token renewal, whichever can be completed safely against current repo contracts

### 20260521-163520

- current task: continue WebSocket/Yjs hardening by fixing high-confidence sync fan-out reliability/performance issues, protocol rejection tests, and SAM throttling guardrails
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/connection-store.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/connection-store.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.yaml`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/README.md`, `package.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, and subagent findings from performance-auditor, security-auditor, and test-auditor
- files changed: `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.yaml`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/README.md`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: sync fan-out had unbounded concurrency, then a bounded limiter that still stopped all remaining peer sends on the first non-Gone failure; expired-peer cleanup ran only after active peer fan-out fully succeeded; Gone cleanup failures were not isolated from other peer delivery; text frames and `messageAuth`/unknown Yjs frames were not explicitly covered; the WebSocket stage lacked route throttling/data-trace guardrails and authorizer/sync reserved concurrency
- security risks found: reduced DoS/cost exposure by adding explicit WebSocket route throttling, disabled API Gateway data tracing, detailed metrics, and authorizer/sync reserved concurrency parameters; reduced CRDT payload integrity risk by rejecting non-base64 text frames before reading the connection store; kept message auth and unknown Yjs frames rejected before DDB/API Gateway calls
- performance issues found: capped peer fan-out and expired cleanup concurrency at 10; changed peer delivery to continue after per-peer non-Gone failures instead of stopping the room-wide queue; made expired cleanup independent so stale records are removed even when active peer delivery has partial or total failures
- validation commands: Prettier on touched WebSocket files; targeted Vitest for `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`; targeted Vitest for WebSocket template/sync; full WebSocket Lambda/template Vitest plus collaboration room-token service test; targeted ESLint for WebSocket template/sync tests and handler; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check` for touched WebSocket files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --audit-level high`; `command -v sam`
- validation results: sync handler test passed 14 tests; WebSocket template/sync tests passed 22 tests; WebSocket Lambda/template suite passed 8 files / 49 tests; TypeScript passed; targeted ESLint passed; diff whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed 542 files / 2682 tests with existing jsdom navigation warnings only; full lint passed; Next.js 16.2.6 production build passed with 217 app routes; dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities; `sam` CLI was still not installed, so SAM build/validate remains unrun
- remaining work: move room-token secret value out of Lambda env into Secrets Manager/SSM SecureString; add actual SAM/cfn-lint deployability gate and possibly Node 20 Lambda test matrix; add client token renewal before 5-minute expiry; consider current-authority revocation/version checks and API Gateway `DeleteConnectionCommand`; consider page-streamed room queries or room connection caps for very large rooms
- next action: continue the broader bug ledger with either Secrets Manager-backed room token secret retrieval or client token renewal, depending on which can be implemented safely with current repo contracts

- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `/Users/yusuke/.codex/memories/MEMORY.md` search results, `tools/infra/websocket` file inventory, `tools/infra/websocket/README.md`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/authorizer/handler.ts`, `vitest.config.ts`, `package.json`, `node_modules/y-websocket/src/y-websocket.js`, and subagent findings for WebSocket connection-store invariants and test coverage
- files changed: `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/connection-store.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/connection-store.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/connect/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/connect/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/disconnect/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/disconnect/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/authorizer/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/authorizer/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/README.md`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, `vitest.config.ts`, `package.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: WebSocket connect/disconnect/sync handlers and connection store were absent even though the README referenced them; sync routing documentation incorrectly implied a JSON `yjs-sync` route despite y-websocket sending binary frames; infra tests under `tools/infra` were excluded from the default Vitest include; connection context needed a second canonical-room check before becoming the sync trust anchor; sync endpoint construction accepted arbitrary requestContext host/stage; sync fan-out ignored DDB pagination and accepted unsupported/oversized payloads
- security risks found: reduced cross-room PHI broadcast risk by making sync load the sender record by `connectionId` and broadcast only to peers in the verified stored room; rejected missing/expired/malformed connection context before storing or broadcasting; added canonical room and entity-type checks at connect; restricted Management API endpoint derivation to execute-api hosts and optional `WEBSOCKET_API_ENDPOINT`; bounded accepted Yjs message types and size; cleaned up expired/stale connection records; added exact authorizer response tests that prevent unverified query room or token leaks into authorizer context
- performance issues found: added room-index DDB pagination so rooms beyond a single Query page are handled; remaining fan-out concurrency is still unbounded and should be capped in a later pass for large rooms
- validation commands: `pnpm add @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb@^3.1024.0 @aws-sdk/client-apigatewaymanagementapi@^3.1024.0` exited 1 after adding packages because existing pnpm build-script approval policy blocked ignored builds; `pnpm install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts`; Prettier on touched WebSocket/token/config files; targeted Vitest for WebSocket authorizer/connect/disconnect/connection-store/sync plus collaboration room-token route/service tests; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; targeted ESLint for touched WebSocket/token/config files; `git diff --check`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`
- validation results: lockfile regenerated without scripts; targeted WebSocket/collaboration Vitest passed 7 files / 30 tests; TypeScript passed; targeted ESLint passed; diff whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed 540 files / 2660 tests with existing jsdom navigation warnings only and now includes `tools/infra/websocket/**/*.test.ts`; full lint passed; Next.js 16.2.6 production build passed with 217 app routes; production dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities
- remaining work: deployable SAM/CDK template is still README-only rather than an executable `template.yaml`; sync fan-out still needs bounded concurrency and optional API Gateway `DeleteConnection` for expired sockets; API Gateway access-log token redaction remains to be implemented and verified; client token renewal before the 5-minute expiry remains a follow-up
- next action: add a real WebSocket SAM template with least-privilege IAM and access-log redaction, then add contract tests to prevent README/template drift

- bugs found: WebSocket `$connect` authorizer implementation was absent despite the README referencing Lambda paths; the authorizer README still described Cognito env settings even though PH-OS room tokens are NextAuth/JWE tokens signed with the application auth secret and custom salt; `issueCollaborationRoomToken` accepted caller-supplied room strings, and token validation did not reject non-canonical signed room/entity combinations; a new verifier regression test was broken by a missing `encode` import

- performance issues found: none severe; token validation is a single JWT/JWE decode on WebSocket connect and does not add per-message DB work
- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prettier --write` on touched token/authorizer/README files; targeted `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.test.ts tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/authorizer/handler.test.ts src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts src/lib/collaboration/yjs-provider.test.ts src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts src/app/api/__tests__/protected-post-routes.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; targeted ESLint for touched token/route/authorizer files; `git diff --check`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`

- remaining work: connect/disconnect/sync Lambda handlers and DynamoDB connection store enforcement are still not implemented, so cross-room broadcast prevention and token-expiry disconnection remain future work; API Gateway access-log token redaction and token renewal after the 5-minute TTL also remain follow-up hardening

- validation commands: `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma format --schema=prisma/schema/`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false db:generate`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/server/services/drug-master-import/manual.test.ts src/app/api/drug-master-imports/manual-clinical/route.test.ts src/app/api/drug-masters/route.test.ts src/app/api/drug-masters/batch/route.test.ts 'src/app/(dashboard)/admin/drug-masters/drug-master-content.test.tsx' src/server/cds/checker.test.ts src/components/features/cds/alert-panel.test.tsx 'src/app/api/patients/[id]/labs/[labId]/route.test.ts'`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`; `git diff --check`; temporary PostgreSQL 17 `initdb` / `pg_ctl start` / `createuser` / `createdb`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false db:e2e:prepare`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false medical-ui:e2e:gate:prod`; temporary PostgreSQL cleanup check

- bugs found: `@sentry/nextjs` needed root-resolvable `@opentelemetry/instrumentation` for config/build; Sentry v10 emitted a deprecated top-level `reactComponentAnnotation` warning; offline crypto re-read IndexedDB keys per payload; active offline page cache name `pages` was also deleted as a legacy cache; prescription drafts, sync queue payloads/conflict payloads, and visit brief cache still used best-effort encryption and could fail open to plaintext PHI; external-access allowed `medication_list`/`allergy_info` scopes for `clerk`; file completion/download authorization did not re-run purpose-specific entity/assignment checks; complete upload did not compare S3 `ContentLength`/`ContentType` to expected metadata
- security risks found: reduced local PHI-at-rest exposure by requiring encrypted prescription draft, sync queue, conflict, SOAP draft, geolog, residual, and visit-brief cache payloads before IndexedDB writes; added migration coverage for legacy plaintext SOAP duplicate fields; reduced external PHI sharing by requiring `canVisit` for medication/allergy external scopes; reduced same-org file IDOR by enforcing purpose-specific assignment checks for complete/download/presigned-download; added S3 size/type verification during complete
- performance issues found: memoized offline CryptoKey in memory to avoid repeated IndexedDB key reads per encrypt/decrypt payload; separated current offline page cache as `offline-pages-v2` so Service Worker activation can delete legacy `pages` without wiping the active offline page cache
- validation commands: targeted Vitest for offline/external-access files; targeted files API/file-storage Vitest; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `pnpm lint`; `pnpm test`; `pnpm build`; `pnpm audit --audit-level moderate`; `git diff --check`

- remaining work: no failing validation remains. Full browser Playwright execution was not rerun in this final integration pass; browser-level shared viewer/offline flow smoke remains the main residual confidence gap. Performance auditor follow-ups not completed here include true bulk `visit-brief` projection, billing evidence bulk upsert/rules memoization, drug-master import index/caching, and Data Explorer search/count optimization.

### 20260521-105717

- current task: fix the High/High dispense barcode verification PHI exposure from the task subagent report
- files inspected: `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.ts`, its tests, `src/server/services/prescription-access.ts`, dispense task/detail/result/queue routes, and Prisma prescription/dispense task schema relations
- files changed: `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/verify-barcode/route.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: `POST /api/dispense-tasks/:id/verify-barcode` had no explicit `canDispense` permission, loaded the dispense task by `{ id, org_id }` only, loaded `PrescriptionLine` by `{ id, org_id }` only, and parsed the submitted barcode before proving the line belonged to the verified task/cycle; this could disclose expected drug code/name for another same-org patient's prescription line
- security risks found: reduced dispensing PHI IDOR by requiring `canDispense`, applying `buildMedicationCycleAssignmentWhere` to the task lookup, constraining `PrescriptionLine` to the verified task's `cycle_id`, and delaying GS1 barcode parsing until after task and line scope are proven
- performance issues found: no material performance issue changed; the line lookup adds a relational cycle predicate and avoids barcode parsing on denied requests

- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, bulk export route/service/job/tests, job route/tests, `package.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `prisma/migrations/20260521094000_rename_audit_functions_to_ph_os/migration.sql`, and three read-only subagent reports for rate-limit, bulk-export, and migration coverage
- files changed: `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.test.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/medications/bulk-export/route.ts`, `src/app/api/patients/medications/bulk-export/route.test.ts`, `src/server/jobs/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.test.ts`, `tools/scripts/verify-ph-os-audit-migration.ts`, `package.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: DynamoDB rate limiting failed open to per-process memory in production and also used import-time cleanup timers unsafe for Edge proxy; NextAuth credentials login used generic write limits instead of strict auth limits; medication history bulk export queue audit was fire-and-forget after job creation and could leave export jobs without audit records; authenticated admins could trigger the bulk-export drain across all organizations; PH-OS audit function migration was not exercised by CI because E2E DB preparation used `prisma db push` instead of running migrations
- security risks found: reduced rate-limit bypass and Edge DoS risk by replacing import-time cleanup with request-time cleanup, failing closed when production distributed rate limiting is unavailable, and routing credentials login attempts through the strict auth limiter; reduced bulk-export audit gaps by writing the export audit inside the queue transaction; reduced cross-tenant side effects by scoping authenticated bulk-export drains to the caller's org while keeping API-key drains global; added a migration CI gate that runs `prisma migrate deploy` and verifies `ph_os_*` audit functions/triggers plus representative Task DML audit behavior
- performance issues found: request-time memory rate-limit cleanup replaces a top-level interval and avoids Edge import side effects; large bulk-export ZIP memory pressure remains a known unfixed issue that requires streaming ZIP/storage design
- validation commands: targeted Vitest for rate-limit, proxy, pdf-bulk-export service, bulk-export route, and job route; targeted ESLint for changed runtime files and verifier script; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `actionlint .github/workflows/ci.yml`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`; `git diff --check`; local `prisma migrate status` probe against `localhost:5433`
- validation results: targeted Vitest passed with 5 files / 41 tests after the final proxy/rate-limit changes; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; actionlint passed; Prisma schema validate passed; full ESLint passed; full Vitest passed with 526 files / 2487 tests; production build passed on Next 16.2.6 with 216 app routes; production dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities; whitespace check passed; local migration status probe could not connect because no PostgreSQL server was running on `localhost:5433`
- remaining work: broader PH-OS Bug Ledger remains active. Remaining items include bulk-export stale running lock recovery, bulk-export completed-job notification failure handling, bulk-export streaming/size-limit memory hardening, external-access medication/allergy policy clarification, and production operational docs/env examples for the new fail-closed DynamoDB rate-limit behavior
- next action: continue with bulk-export stale lock recovery and completion notification failure handling, which are high-confidence service-level defects with focused tests

### 20260521-120658

- current task: fix bulk-export final artifact audit and add a bounded memory guard before synchronous ZIP generation
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.test.ts`, `src/server/services/export-audit.ts`, `src/server/services/export-audit.test.ts`, `src/server/services/file-storage.ts`, and two read-only subagent reports for final audit and memory guard placement
- files changed: `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: successful medication-history bulk export jobs wrote only the request-time export audit and did not audit the actual ZIP artifact, file ID, success count, or failed count; partial-success jobs had no audit record showing the actual successful PDF count; final audit failures could otherwise become completed jobs without an artifact audit if not coupled to the completion update; `zipSync` could run after rendering a very large set of PDFs with no total byte guard, causing a large synchronous memory/CPU spike before storage
- security risks found: improved PHI export auditability by writing final artifact audit in the same DB transaction as the completed job update; final audit failure now prevents ready notification and leaves the job in failed handling rather than exposing an unaudited completed export
- performance issues found: added `MEDICATION_HISTORY_BULK_EXPORT_MAX_TOTAL_PDF_BYTES` with a 128 MiB default guard before `zipSync` and `storeGeneratedFile`, preventing the largest avoidable synchronous ZIP allocation for oversized rendered PDFs; render-phase memory pressure remains a larger streaming-design follow-up

- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, Next 16 proxy docs, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/request-ip.ts`, `src/lib/api/request-ip.test.ts`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, `src/app/api/health/route.ts`, `src/app/api/health/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/backup-monitor.ts`, `.env.example`, and three read-only subagent reports for rate-limit security/test/correctness

- bugs found: production DynamoDB rate-limit calls could hang proxy execution with no timeout; quota exceeded, store unavailable, and store misconfiguration were all reported as indistinguishable 429s; `/api/health` could be blocked by the same fail-closed limiter it should help observe; production unauthenticated requests without a resolvable client IP collapsed into shared `ip:unknown` buckets; public health calls ran expensive backup/AWS diagnostics before admin authorization; deploy env docs did not mention required production rate-limit/DDB settings
- security risks found: reduced global lockout/bypass ambiguity by rejecting production unauthenticated API requests when no trusted client IP can be resolved, validating forwarded IP literals, supporting `TRUSTED_PROXY_HOPS`, preserving reason metadata for security events, and documenting production-only distributed rate-limit env requirements; public health no longer triggers backup/AWS diagnostics for anonymous callers
- performance issues found: added `RATE_LIMIT_DDB_TIMEOUT_MS` with a 1500ms default AbortController timeout so proxy rate-limit checks fail closed instead of hanging; split public health into cheap DB liveness while admin callers still receive backup diagnostics

### 20260521-131300

- current task: implement bulk-export artifact TTL, scheduled cleanup, and lifecycle/object-lock alignment from subagent findings
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/file-storage.ts`, `src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts`, `src/server/services/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/jobs/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/jobs/index.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/route.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/route.test.ts`, `tools/infra/eventbridge-schedules.json`, `tools/infra/prescription-object-lock.json`, `docs/compliance/data-classification.md`, and three read-only subagent reports for TTL/cleanup/heartbeat risks
- files changed: `src/server/services/file-storage.ts`, `src/server/services/file-storage.test.ts`, `src/server/jobs/pdf-bulk-export.ts`, `src/server/jobs/index.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/[jobType]/route.test.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/route.ts`, `src/app/api/jobs/route.test.ts`, `tools/infra/eventbridge-schedules.json`, `tools/infra/file-storage-lifecycle.json`, `tools/infra/prescription-object-lock.json`, `docs/compliance/data-classification.md`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: generated bulk-export ZIP metadata had no durable expiry marker; expired generated ZIPs were not rejected at download time; the existing generated-file cleanup helper had no job handler or schedule; naive `file_asset:*` scanning could starve expired files behind non-bulk/unexpired settings; shared bucket object-lock template configured a bucket-level default retention that could make temporary bulk exports undeletable; S3 lifecycle coverage for `bulk-exports/` was missing; job route did not explicitly test surfacing drain errors
- security risks found: reduced PHI ZIP retention risk by adding explicit `expiresAt` for new bulk-export artifacts, rejecting expired downloads with `FILE_EXPIRED`, adding paged cleanup of expired bulk-export metadata/S3 objects, wiring API-key scheduled cleanup, adding a `bulk-exports/` S3 lifecycle backstop, and removing bucket-level default object-lock retention from the shared file bucket template so temporary exports remain deletable
- performance issues found: bounded cleanup batch/page sizes while allowing cursor pagination across later settings so expired files are not permanently hidden behind active metadata; added `scannedCount` output for operational visibility

### 20260521-140153

- current task: remove notification SSE proxy rate-limit bypass and harden SSE connection cleanup from subagent findings
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/proxy.ts`, `src/proxy.test.ts`, `src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.ts`, `src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/server/adapters/realtime/redis-adapter.ts`, notification/realtime client call sites referenced by subagents, and read-only security/performance/test/reviewer subagent reports for SSE rate-limit and lifecycle behavior

- bugs found: `GET /api/notifications/stream` bypassed the distributed proxy rate limiter entirely and relied only on process-local SSE connection gauges; SSE route tests did not cover connection-cap 429, release-on-abort/cancel, or gauge semantics; the stream route could leave realtime listeners/counters active when a request was aborted during subscription, when a stream body was cancelled, or when Redis registered a listener before subscribe failed
- security risks found: reduced availability abuse by making stream opens consume the normal read rate budget before route auth/realtime work; kept `/api/health` as the only rate-limit-exempt API path; preserved route auth and per-user SSE cap; residual org-wide realtime metadata exposure remains unfixed because removing or redacting the org channel affects realtime workflow API/UI semantics
- performance issues found: reduced unauthenticated/over-budget stream-open amplification at the proxy; reduced listener/counter leaks by adding idempotent teardown, abort listener removal, `ReadableStream.cancel()` cleanup, and tracked/idempotent subscription cleanup for late success or partial failure
- validation commands: targeted `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/proxy.test.ts src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.test.ts src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`; targeted ESLint for proxy/stream/rate-limit files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; `git diff --check`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`

- bugs found: QR draft confirmation emitted `qr_draft_confirmed` but the prescription workspace ignored it, so open prescription intake lists could stay stale after another user confirmed a QR draft; `POST /api/prescription-intakes` with `qr_draft_id` also marked the QR draft as confirmed without emitting any QR confirmation event; presence realtime remains mismatched because the API publishes to `presence:*` while `/api/notifications/stream` does not subscribe to that channel family, but widening presence to org-wide realtime is unsafe before entity-level authorization is added
- security risks found: fixed QR freshness using type-only `qr_draft_confirmed` events and existing authorized refetches, without adding draft/intake/patient/case identifiers to realtime payloads; did not route presence payloads through org-wide realtime because presence includes PHI-adjacent operational metadata and the presence endpoint currently accepts arbitrary entity ids behind only `canDispense`
- performance issues found: no meaningful performance issue; QR confirmation now triggers targeted React Query invalidation for `['prescription-intakes', orgId]` instead of waiting for navigation or unrelated refresh

### 20260521-151704

- current task: close high-confidence presence authz/channel boundary issues without moving presence to org-wide realtime
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/app/api/presence/route.ts`, `src/app/api/presence/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/presence-store.ts`, `src/server/services/org-realtime-policy.test.ts`, `src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/components/features/collaboration/presence-avatars.tsx`, `src/lib/auth/context.ts`, `src/lib/auth/permissions.ts`, `src/lib/auth/middleware.ts`, `src/lib/auth/visit-schedule-access.ts`, `src/server/services/prescription-access.ts`, `src/app/api/dispense-tasks/[id]/route.ts`, and read-only security/test subagent reports for presence authz/realtime gaps
- files changed: `src/app/api/presence/route.ts`, `src/app/api/presence/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/org-realtime-policy.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: `/api/presence` accepted arbitrary `entity_type`/`entity_id` behind only `canDispense`, so same-org users could write/read presence for unassigned or unsupported entities before any entity-level access check; presence realtime channel omitted `orgId`, so identical entity ids in different organizations could collide on the adapter channel; presence heartbeat awaited realtime broadcast directly, so a Redis/realtime outage could turn best-effort presence updates into failed POSTs after the in-memory presence write
- security risks found: reduced same-org-but-unassigned PHI metadata exposure by allowlisting presence entities to `dispense_task` and `visit_record`, checking org plus assignment-scoped access with existing medication-cycle and visit-schedule helpers before store reads/writes/broadcasts, returning before side effects on unsupported/inaccessible entities, and keeping presence off org-wide realtime; reduced cross-tenant channel collision risk with `presence:${orgId}:${entity_type}:${entity_id}` while preserving policy tests that forbid org-wide presence publishing
- performance issues found: no meaningful regression; the route adds one bounded entity lookup before presence read/write and keeps realtime broadcast best-effort
- validation commands: targeted `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/app/api/presence/route.test.ts src/server/services/org-realtime-policy.test.ts`; targeted `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/app/api/presence/route.test.ts src/server/services/org-realtime-policy.test.ts src/app/api/notifications/stream/route.test.ts src/server/services/org-realtime.test.ts src/server/services/workflow-dashboard-cache.test.ts`; targeted ESLint for presence/policy files; targeted Prettier check for presence/policy files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; targeted `git diff --check`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --prod --audit-level moderate`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec prisma validate --schema=prisma/schema/`

### 20260521-170556

- current task: fix Yjs collaboration room token client renewal and fail-closed handling for authorization loss
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx`, `src/lib/collaboration/yjs-provider.ts`, `src/lib/collaboration/form-yjs-bridge.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, `src/lib/api/response.ts`, y-websocket provider implementation/types, and read-only reviewer/test/security subagent reports for collaboration token lifecycle
- files changed: `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: client fetched a 5-minute collaboration room token once and never renewed it; reconnects after token expiry would keep using stale provider credentials; token renewal denial was initially treated like a transient retry, leaving the old provider alive until server-side expiry; room-token API returned a secret-bearing JSON response without explicit no-store headers
- security risks found: reduced stale authorization exposure for honest clients by destroying the active provider on 4xx renewal denial and setting awareness/connected state to disconnected; kept 429/5xx/network failures as transient retry without immediately dropping an otherwise still-valid provider; added no-store/no-cache headers to the room-token response
- performance issues found: token refresh scheduling is one timer per active collaborative form, with a 60-second refresh skew and 5-second retry floor; no unbounded loop introduced

- validation results: targeted hook test passed with 1 file / 4 tests; targeted hook+route tests passed with 2 files / 7 tests; related collaboration/WebSocket suite passed with 8 files / 55 tests; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed with 542 files / 2693 tests; full ESLint passed; production build passed on Next 16.2.6 with 217 app routes; production dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities

### 20260521-175351

- current task: harden collaboration room-token transient retry behavior with `Retry-After`, bounded exponential backoff, and deterministic regression coverage
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, and read-only reviewer/test/security subagent reports for room-token retry/backoff behavior
- files changed: `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.ts`, `src/lib/hooks/use-collaborative-form.test.tsx`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: 429/5xx/network token fetch failures retried on a fixed 5-second interval; 429/503 `Retry-After` headers were ignored; collaboration token unavailable 503 responses did not tell clients how long to back off; several hook tests used absolute token expiry timestamps that would become expired as wall-clock time advanced
- security risks found: reduced endpoint pressure and retry amplification against the room-token/rate-limit path by respecting `Retry-After` and adding bounded exponential backoff with jitter; kept non-429 4xx responses terminal so authorization denials still fail closed without retrying or preserving presence side effects
- performance issues found: reduced synchronized retry storms by replacing fixed 5-second transient retries with 5s/10s/... bounded exponential delays plus jitter, and by adding `Retry-After: 60` to known server-side token-unavailable 503 responses

- validation results: targeted hook/provider/route Vitest passed with 3 files / 22 tests; related collaboration suite passed with 8 files / 81 tests; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; targeted whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed with 543 files / 2708 tests; full ESLint passed; production build passed on Next 16.2.6 with 217 app routes; dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities

### 20260521-181903

- current task: close stale WebSocket connections from the sync Lambda when connection records are expired, gone, or already missing from the verified connection store
- files inspected: `git status --short`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/shared/connection-store.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/template.yaml`, and read-only security/test subagent reports for stale WebSocket cleanup and Management API test coverage
- files changed: `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.ts`, `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: expired sender/peer and Gone peer cleanup removed DDB records but did not close API Gateway WebSocket connections; missing sender records returned 403 without best-effort socket close; initial tests only checked command constructor calls and did not prove `DeleteConnectionCommand` was sent through the Management API client; API Gateway close failure behavior was not locked as best-effort
- security risks found: reduced stale socket resource-abuse risk by best-effort closing expired, gone, and missing WebSocket connections while keeping DDB connection records as the sync authorization authority; non-Gone post failures are still not treated as stale and are not closed
- performance issues found: close calls reuse the existing bounded fan-out/cleanup concurrency path for expired peers; close failures are logged as sanitized counts without connection IDs or PHI
- validation commands: Prettier on changed sync files; targeted Vitest for `tools/infra/websocket/lambdas/sync/handler.test.ts`; related WebSocket Vitest for sync, connection-store, connect, disconnect, authorizer, room-token, template, and app collaboration room-token service tests; targeted ESLint for changed sync files; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; targeted `git diff --check`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false lint`; full `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false audit --audit-level high`
- validation results: sync handler Vitest passed with 1 file / 16 tests; related WebSocket suite passed with 8 files / 59 tests; targeted ESLint passed; TypeScript passed; targeted whitespace check passed; full Vitest passed with 543 files / 2712 tests; full ESLint passed; production build passed on Next 16.2.6 with 217 app routes; dependency audit reported no known vulnerabilities
- remaining work: broader PH-OS Bug Ledger remains active. Remaining collaboration follow-ups include server-side revocation/session-version checks for already-open sockets, per-room/per-user WebSocket connection caps or soft limits, provider candidate timeout/connection-error handling beyond status `disconnected`, in-flight room-token request dedupe across simultaneous hook instances, stable identity for field-array collaboration keys, and initial Yjs default-value seeding
- next action: continue with server-side revocation/session-version design or the smaller room/user connection cap, depending on acceptable infrastructure scope

- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/conference-data-sync.ts`, `src/server/services/conference-data-sync.test.ts`, `src/server/jobs/daily-helpers.ts`, `src/server/jobs/daily-helpers.test.ts`, `src/server/jobs/monthly.ts`, `src/server/jobs/monthly.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`

- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.ts`, `src/server/services/collaboration-room-token.test.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.ts`, `src/app/api/collaboration/room-token/route.test.ts`, `src/lib/visits/facility-visit-context.ts`, `src/lib/visits/facility-visit-context.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`

- bugs found: `decodeFacilityVisitContext` retried `decodeURIComponent(raw)` recursively on any parse failure; for invalid non-encoded strings, the decoded value equals the original, causing unbounded recursion instead of returning null. - security risks found: legacy/shared URL context is now object-checked before restoring grouped visit UI state; malformed arrays/scalars are ignored, and invalid raw query values fail closed without recursive decode loops. No auth/authz behavior changed
- performance issues found: fixed an unbounded recursion path on malformed input; normal successful decode remains a single JSON parse plus bounded patient normalization

### 20260531-131848

- current task: harden offline sync queue payload decoding before network sync and conflict handling
- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/stores/sync-engine.ts`, `src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, `src/lib/stores/offline-db.ts`, sync engine call sites in visit record and schedule views
- files changed: `src/lib/stores/sync-engine.ts`, `src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: `processSyncQueue()` used `(await decryptOfflinePayload(item.payload)) ?? item.payload` directly as the POST body, so malformed encrypted queue payloads or legacy non-object strings could be sent to API routes instead of being contained to the queue item. Conflict handling also reparsed the decrypted payload with a loose generic cast when building the local conflict snapshot
- security risks found: offline sync payloads now must decode to a JSON object before being sent to server endpoints or used in conflict overwrite flows. Invalid payloads are marked failed with `Invalid sync payload`, conflict metadata is cleared, and no malformed body is sent over the network. No auth/authz behavior changed
- performance issues found: no meaningful runtime performance issue found; validation adds one bounded JSON parse/object check per queued item before existing network I/O
- validation commands: `pnpm exec prettier --write src/lib/stores/sync-engine.ts src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec eslint src/lib/stores/sync-engine.ts src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts src/lib/db/json.ts --max-warnings=0`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec vitest run src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts`; `rg -n "body: \\(await decryptOfflinePayload|readSyncPayload|Invalid sync payload|not-json|parseJson<Record<string, unknown>>\\(await decryptOfflinePayload\\(item\\.payload\\)" src/lib/stores/sync-engine.ts src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts`; `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check -- src/lib/stores/sync-engine.ts src/lib/stores/sync-engine.test.ts`
- validation results: formatting passed; targeted ESLint passed with zero warnings; targeted Vitest passed with 1 file / 3 tests; grep confirmed direct decrypted payload POST bodies and the old conflict local-payload parse path are gone, while `readSyncPayload` and malformed payload coverage exist; TypeScript passed; whitespace check passed

Malformed arrays, mixed hash arrays, and missing timestamps are rejected without updating or deleting the stored setting. - files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/server/adapters/e-prescription/index.ts`, `src/server/adapters/e-prescription/index.test.ts`, `src/server/adapters/http-client.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, e-prescription route/caller references found by `rg`, current git status

- bugs found: `MhlwEPrescriptionAdapter.fetchPrescription()` and `searchPrescriptions()` returned unwrapped successful JSON as `EPrescriptionRecord`/`EPrescriptionRecord[]` without validating record or medication-line shape. A successful response with invalid status, malformed dates, mixed item arrays, non-string patient IDs, invalid quantity, arrays/null/scalars, or malformed search rows could reach prescription import callers as trusted prescription data
- security risks found: e-prescription success responses now fail closed unless each record is a JSON object with non-empty prescription/patient IDs, valid issued/expires dates, allowed status, nullable string names, optional typed refill metadata, optional object raw payload, and medication items with positive integer line numbers, non-empty drug/dose/frequency fields, positive finite days, nullable finite quantity, nullable unit, and optional string/null notes. Malformed successful fetch/search responses raise `EPrescriptionAdapterError` with `UPSTREAM_FAILURE`. ### 20260531-143320

- current task: harden offline sync visit-record conflict snapshot parsing before overwrite/conflict persistence

- bugs found: offline sync conflict snapshots were parsed with a generic `JSON.parse(...) as VisitRecordConflictSnapshot`, and 409 response bodies were read through object casts. Malformed `existing_record` payloads or stored conflict snapshots with invalid `server.id/version` could be persisted or used when building overwrite requests
- security risks found: decrypted conflict snapshots and HTTP 409 conflict response bodies now fail closed unless the top-level conflict is an object with object `local` payload and a valid nullable server snapshot. Server snapshots require string identifiers, integer version, string patient/date/status fields, typed optional SOAP fields, and typed residual-medication rows. Malformed stored conflicts now stop overwrite before network submission, reducing accidental clinical-record overwrite risk. No encryption helper, queue endpoint, retry policy, or draft deletion behavior changed

### 20260531-144157

- current task: harden DynamoDB rate-limit credential/counter response parsing before auth/API quota decisions
- files inspected: `.codex/ralph-state.md`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `src/lib/db/json.ts`, `src/lib/aws/sigv4.ts`, rate-limit and AWS references found by `rg`, current git status
- files changed: `src/lib/api/rate-limit.ts`, `src/lib/api/rate-limit.test.ts`, `.codex/ralph-state.md`
- bugs found: DynamoDB rate-limit code parsed container credentials and DynamoDB `UpdateItem` counters with type assertions. Malformed container credential fields could reach SigV4 signing, and malformed `hit_count`/`reset_at` values could become `NaN` in quota results instead of failing the distributed store path cleanly. TypeScript also caught that optional container `Token` parsing could pass `null` into `AwsCredentials.sessionToken`
- security risks found: container credentials now fail closed unless the credentials payload is a JSON object with string access key and secret key plus typed optional token/expiration. DynamoDB update responses now fail closed unless `Attributes.hit_count.N` and `Attributes.reset_at.N` are finite numeric strings. In production, malformed credentials/counters now produce `store_unavailable` rather than allowing malformed quota state. No credential URL allowlist, request signing, static credential fallback ordering, production deny-all guard, route canonicalization behavior, or DynamoDB request body changed in this slice
- performance issues found: no meaningful performance issue found; validation is bounded to one credentials payload and one DynamoDB response object
